tips: Use RESTful naming: plural nouns for resources (/users, /posts)
Return proper HTTP status codes — 200 OK, 201 Created, 400 Bad Request, 404 Not Found, 500 Server Error
Version your API from day one — /v1/users prevents breaking changes later
Use query parameters for filtering, sorting, and searching
Return consistent error responses with a message and code field
Rate limit your APIs to prevent abuse and ensure fair usage
Use idempotency keys for mutating operations to safely retry requests
Document your API with OpenAPI/Swagger for developer experience
Use conditional requests with ETags for efficient caching
Validate all inputs server-side even if you validate client-side too
Use CORS headers to control which domains can access your API
